rung: W-2 causal block-commit verify redesign + drafter boundary-trim
object: twin@plain twinK1, FLARE hybrid_clean, mask 248077, maxlen 32768, gmu 0.74, seqs 4, BIDIR_PROBE=1; gate VLLM_FASTDLLM_W1_DRAFT_VERIFY OFF(ref)/ON(W-2)

design.verify_forward: CAUSAL (forced in prepare_attn for _hc_verify slots; kills the bidir full-reveal leak) + FIXED block-commit width (reuses the commit's captured cudagraph -> deterministic replay, removes the W-1d variable-width numeric fork) + read-only (is_encoder_phase False -> snapshot/restored, zero GDN advance)
design.accept: PREFIX-COMMIT: longest prefix m where causal-argmax==draft; commit draft[:m], divergent position K=1 (one-step cooldown); byte-assert retained (fail-closed)
design.boundary_trim: grammar_clip G5 hook trims the drafted span at the first content/scaffold boundary (fence/special) before staging (the 83%-over-copy lever)
design.architectural_finding: the FLARE commit folds the whole block to ONE end-state (postprocess_state feeds neutral num_accepted=1); there are NO per-position GDN states to select, so literal 'ride the commit forward, zero extra forward, prefix-commit' is not jointly realizable. Realized: a fixed-block-WIDTH causal read-only verify forward that reuses the commit's captured graph (deterministic) + prefix-commit via the existing deferred block-commit fold.

gate_on_W2.note: idx4 (W-1d 4/5 flip) now 5/5 -> BAR MET by construction

throughput.vs_W1b: 3.32x blended > W-1b 2.35x/1.95x; reject share 11.1% partial vs W-1d 94.8%

live_recert.unfired_byte_parity: gate-ON committed stream == gate-OFF K=1 across the full 30-req corpus (30/30 exact each vs same gold, det_on==det_off)

verdict_reason: gate-ON now matches gate-OFF reproducibility (6/6) AND exactness (30/30) with arej=0 and 0 false accepts, at 3.32x blended forwards-speedup. The W-1d numeric non-reproducibility is resolved by the causal fixed-width read-only verify; the boundary-trim + prefix-commit cut the reject tax from 94.8% to 11.1% partial / 0% full.
